detaileddescription.para: This should be called when a thread has encountered an unrecoverable runtime condition and needs to terminate. What this ultimately means is determined by the _fatal_error_handler() implementation, which will be called with reason code K_ERR_KERNEL_OOPS.
If this is called from ISR context, the default system fatal error handler will treat it as an unrecoverable system error, just like k_panic().

This should be called when the Zephyr kernel has encountered an unrecoverable runtime condition and needs to terminate. What this ultimately means is determined by the _fatal_error_handler() implementation, which will be called with reason code K_ERR_KERNEL_PANIC.

briefdescription.para: Initialize the specified IPI work item.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_SCHED_IPI_SUPPORTED.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the IPI work item to be initialized

briefdescription.para: Add an IPI work item to the IPI work queue.

detaileddescription.para: Adds the specified IPI work item to the IPI work queues of each CPU identified by cpu_bitmask. The specified IPI work item will subsequently execute at ISR level as those CPUs process their received IPIs. Do not re-use the specified IPI work item until it has been processed by all of the identified CPUs.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_SCHED_IPI_SUPPORTED.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the IPI work item
Set of CPUs to which the IPI work item will be sent
Function to execute on the targeted CPU(s)
if the specified IPI work item is still being processed

briefdescription.para: Wait until the IPI work item has been processed by all targeted CPUs.

detaileddescription.para: This routine waits until the IPI work item has been processed by all CPUs to which it was sent. If called from an ISR, then timeout must be set to K_NO_WAIT. To prevent deadlocks the caller must not have IRQs locked when calling this function.

simplesect.para: It is not in general possible to poll safely for completion of this function in ISR or locked contexts where the calling CPU cannot service IPIs (because the targeted CPUs may themselves be waiting on the calling CPU). Application code must be prepared for failure or to poll from a thread context.
Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_SCHED_IPI_SUPPORTED.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the IPI work item
Maximum time to wait for IPI work to be processed
if processed by all targeted CPUs

briefdescription.para: Signal that there is one or more IPI work items to process.

detaileddescription.para: This routine sends an IPI to the set of CPUs identified by calls to k_ipi_work_add() since this CPU sent its last set of IPIs.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_SCHED_IPI_SUPPORTED.

briefdescription.para: Get the runtime statistics of a thread.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to struct to copy statistics into.

briefdescription.para: Get the runtime statistics of all threads.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to struct to copy statistics into.

briefdescription.para: Get the runtime statistics of all threads on specified cpu.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to struct to copy statistics into.

briefdescription.para: Enable gathering of runtime statistics for specified thread.

detaileddescription.para: This routine enables the gathering of runtime statistics for the specified thread.

briefdescription.para: Disable gathering of runtime statistics for specified thread.

detaileddescription.para: This routine disables the gathering of runtime statistics for the specified thread.

briefdescription.para: Enable gathering of system runtime statistics.

detaileddescription.para: This routine enables the gathering of system runtime statistics. Note that it does not affect the gathering of similar statistics for individual threads.

briefdescription.para: Disable gathering of system runtime statistics.

detaileddescription.para: This routine disables the gathering of system runtime statistics. Note that it does not affect the gathering of similar statistics for individual threads.
